title: Fix MongoDB Aggregation Pipeline to Handle Nested Fields with Optional Keys and Null Field Names

statement.markdown: Implement a function to fix the MongoDB aggregation pipeline to correctly handle nested fields with optional keys and null field names. The function should take an array of documents and return the fixed pipeline.
statement.inputFormat: Array of documents where each document is an object with nested fields
statement.outputFormat: Fixed MongoDB aggregation pipeline as an array of objects
statement.notes: The pipeline should correctly handle fields with optional keys and null field names. The function should also skip unnecessary fields in the projection.

constraints.description: Number of documents in the input array

examples.explanation: The output pipeline correctly handles nested fields with optional keys and null field names

hints.text: Use the $ifNull operator to handle null field names

editorial.title: Editorial: Fixing MongoDB Aggregation Pipeline
editorial.intuition: The correct approach is to use the $ifNull operator to handle null field names and the $project operator to skip unnecessary fields in the projection.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach involves manually writing each pipeline stage for each document
editorial.betterSolution: Use a recursive function to generate the pipeline stages
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a recursive function to generate the pipeline stages and the $ifNull operator to handle null field names
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness involves verifying that the output pipeline correctly handles nested fields with optional keys and null field names
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run of the optimal solution
commonMistakes: Failing to handle null field names
Skipping unnecessary fields in the projection

starterCode.javascript: // Define a recursive function to generate the pipeline stages
